question: Does GMP extend to the full local algebra, or is the scope limitation irreducible? If irreducible, characterize the exact complement outside the realized typed bridge class.

typed_bridge_class.definition: The realized bridge/readout class fixed by Paper 32 Theorem 32.B and Paper 32 Theorem 32.E: source/readout operators on the active typed bridge architecture, with tensor type, channel floor, background placement, and multiplicity-one bridge grammar fixed.
typed_bridge_class.gmp_statement: Inside this class, puncture-load-to-channel assignment is forced geometrically.

classes.name: recombination_history_block
classes.claim: Physical and realized, but not a bridge-placement operator class. GMP is inapplicable.

classes.name: reionization_local_history_block
classes.claim: Function-valued local history block. Physical and realized, but no puncture-load/channel placement datum.

classes.name: closed_S3_perturbation_block
classes.claim: Physical and realized perturbation hierarchy downstream of source plus thermodynamic history. Not itself a new bridge-placement operator.

abstract_nongeometric_complement.claim: The full local algebra still contains explicit non-geometric bypass operators with identity spatial leg; universal GMP is therefore false on the larger algebra.
abstract_nongeometric_complement.example: Q_w^(ng) = R_w tensor I_th tensor I_sp tensor sqrt(L1) P_1

final_classification.bridge_placement_observables: typed bridge class is exhaustive for physically realized boundary-to-bulk bridge-placement observables
final_classification.physical_outside_typed_class: yes, but they are non-bridge history/solver observables, so GMP is not the right predicate there

future_ai_must_not_say: Universal GMP is derived on the full local algebra.
Nothing physical lives outside the typed bridge class.
The abstract identity-spatial-leg bypass class was removed by Paper 32.
The Stage-2 or reionization history blocks are extra GMP counterexamples rather than non-bridge classes.
